message: 🌱 Add istio: CNI Repair mode not working in 1.26.1 mission (#499)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "istio-56587-cni-repair-mode-not-working-in-1-26-1",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "istio: CNI Repair mode not working in 1.26.1",
+    "description": "CNI Repair mode not working in 1.26.1. This issue affects 20+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify istio troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your istio deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio -n istio --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for error: `Error connecting to 127.0.0.6:15002: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:0->127.0.0.6:15002: connect: connection refused`"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review istio configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant istio configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio\nkubectl get configmap -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=istio\n```\n### Is this the right place to submit this?\n\n### Bug Description\n\nWe've just upgraded from 1.25.1 to 1.26.1 and have noticed that the Istio CNI repair mode does not seem to (always?) be working correctly."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for CNI Repair mode not working in 1.26.1",
+        "description": "**Please provide a description of this PR:**\n\nWhen IPv4 was in a clean state requiring new iptables, but IPv6 had a state with compatible residues (n.b.: user-generated rules are considered residues too), the IPv6 result was preferred over the other.\n```yaml\n### Additional Information\n\nistio-cni-node logs:\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm CNI Repair mode not working in 1.26.1 is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio -n istio --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n istio --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that `Error connecting to 127.0.0.6:15002: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:0->127.0.0.6:15002: connect: connection refused` no longer appears in logs."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "**Please provide a description of this PR:**\n\nWhen IPv4 was in a clean state requiring new iptables, but IPv6 had a state with compatible residues (n.b.: user-generated rules are considered residues too), the IPv6 result was preferred over the other.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "### Additional Information\n\nistio-cni-node logs:",
+        "Pod validation container logs",
+        "**Please provide a description of this PR:**\r\n\r\nWhen IPv4 was in a clean state requiring new iptables, but IPv6 had a state with compatible residues (n.b.: user-generated rules are considered residues too), the IPv6 result was preferred over the other.\r\nFixes #56587\ncc @leosarra \nLooks like the pod did not have any iptables at all and the repair logic ended up not applying new iptables rules because the existing ones were consistent with the desired state (which I doubt is empty). This is weird.\n@hamishforbes can you provide the debug logs from the istio cni node agent, it may shed some light\n> Looks like the pod did not have any iptables at all and the repair logic ended up not applying new iptables rules because the existing ones were consistent with the desired state (which I doubt is e"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
